H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H‑OS‑L1 (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong) attains 66 % positives; listeners report noticeable improvement during commuting or relaxation, with ~10‑minute settle time. Reach is higher (30 views) but retention data are absent. AutoGluon predicts 6.86, close to observed.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: A popular choice for overstimulation, praised for its calming rhythm during daily activities.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H‑OS‑L2 (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong) has 65 % positives; qualitative notes include slight improvements and mixed music opinions. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon’s prediction (6.86) aligns with the mean.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Users report modest calming effects, with some preferring other tracks.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H‑OS‑L3 (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) records 64 % positives; listeners often use it to aid sleep, reporting balanced rhythms. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon predicts 6.86, consistent with observed performance.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Generally helpful for winding down, especially before sleep.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H‑AX‑L1 (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong) shows solid anxiety reduction; 63 % report positive outcomes. Qualitative feedback highlights a ~10‑minute settle time and balanced rhythm appreciation. YouTube reach is minimal (7 views, no retention data). AutoGluon predicts a higher efficacy score (7.84), suggesting untapped potential in unseen listeners.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners generally feel calmer after about ten minutes, and the track seems especially effective for anxiety.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H‑AX‑L2 (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) retains a 67 % positive rate with mixed music opinions. Listeners settle in ~5 minutes and report moderate improvement. YouTube views are 6, offering negligible external signal. AutoGluon’s predicted score (≈7.84 for anxiety) aligns closely with observed performance.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Most users notice a moderate lift in mood, with a quick settle time.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: H‑AX‑L3 (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful) achieves 61 % positive responses; qualitative notes emphasize noticeable improvement and a balanced rhythm. Settlement occurs around 10 minutes. YouTube data (4 views) are insufficient for inference. AutoGluon prediction mirrors the observed mean.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: The protocol feels soothing and balances well, helping many reduce stress.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: H‑CR‑L1 (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) records 53 % positives, with many noting significant improvement despite lower mean typical of crisis protocols. Listeners settle within 5‑10 minutes. YouTube reach is 6 views. AutoGluon forecasts a higher score (7.96 for crisis), indicating possible under‑capture of benefit.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Even in acute distress, people report noticeable relief fairly quickly.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: H‑CR‑L2 (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) shows 55 % positive outcomes; qualitative data note fast settle times (~5 minutes) but mixed music sentiment. YouTube views are 4. AutoGluon predicts 7.96, again exceeding observed mean.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners find fast help during crisis moments, though music opinions vary.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H‑CR‑L3 (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful) yields 52 % positives; distribution hints at bimodality, suggesting distinct responder groups. Settlement is often immediate. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon predicts 7.96, a sizable upward gap.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Feelings of improvement are mixed, hinting at different reactions among users.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: H‑SL‑L1 (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong) achieves 65 % positives; rapid settle (~5 minutes) is common. YouTube reach is low (4 views). AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for sleep, slightly above observed mean. Notable embedding drift (similarity = 0.649) suggests recent analytic shift.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Many find it settles them quickly for better sleep, though opinions differ on the music.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: H‑SL‑L2 (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful) shows 67 % positives; clinician‑driven use is high, though a minority report no effect. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon prediction matches the mean. Drift is moderate (similarity = 0.853).
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Clinicians often use this, and most listeners notice a pleasant restfulness.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: H‑SL‑L3 (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) yields 64 % positives; settlement occurs within 5‑10 minutes. YouTube views are 3. AutoGluon prediction (7.54) aligns closely. Drift similarity is 0.856.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Provides a gentle push toward rest, with a few users seeing little change.

T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Helps focus for many, though some only see slight gains.

T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Most users feel sharper and appreciate the rhythm.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: T‑CF‑L1 (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful) records 53 % positives; listeners note immediate or early settle and balanced rhythm. YouTube views are 2. AutoGluon predicts 6.86 for low‑intensity, close to observed.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The dataset shows generally positive listener outcomes with systematic differences across the three series.
• HEALING protocols achieve the highest consistency (std≈1.8) and largest sample size, indicating stable regulatory effects.
• THRIVING protocols display slightly higher mean scores, especially in Focus & Clarity, while maintaining comparable variability.
• TRANSFORMING protocols exhibit the lowest mean and the widest spread, reflecting the intended challenge of ascending‑arc designs.
• YouTube reach is uniformly low (≤30 views per protocol), limiting external validation of efficacy signals.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series‑level patterns align with design intentions.
• HEALING leads on response reliability, with the lowest variance and the strongest confidence tiers across most entries.
• THRIVING peaks in Focus & Clarity (e.g., T‑FC‑L3) and often outperforms HEALING on mean scores despite similar variability.
• TRANSFORMING shows the broadest outcome distribution, with occasional high‑scoring outliers (e.g., X‑MD‑L3) amid generally lower averages.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A few protocols deviate from series‑wide trends and merit closer scrutiny.
• H‑CR‑L3 exhibits a potentially bimodal distribution, suggesting distinct listener sub‑populations.
• X‑MC‑L3 records the lowest mean (5.7) and high standard deviation, hinting at a mismatch between protocol demands and listener state.
• Multiple protocols (e.g., H‑SL‑L1, T‑MB‑L3) show notable embedding drift (similarity < 0.80), which may signal evolving response patterns or analytical instability.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted adjustments can sharpen efficacy and data quality.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑MC‑L3 (n=10) – investigate population mismatch and consider redesigning Hz parameters or movement arc.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑CR‑L3 (n=27) – explore subgroup segmentation to explain bimodality; possibly tailor adviser routing.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Protocols with embedding drift < 0.80 (e.g., H‑SL‑L1, T‑MB‑L3, X‑MD‑L3) – audit recent calibration changes and re‑evaluate feature pipelines.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Low YouTube reach across the board – pilot targeted promotion to improve discovery and gather richer retention data.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑AX‑L1 (n=67) – test an L2 variant with adjusted duration to support the minority of listeners reporting worsening effects.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding signals reveal both stability and change across runs.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: 18 protocols have similarity < 0.85, notably H‑SL‑L1, T‑MB‑L3, X‑MD‑L3, indicating evolving analytic patterns.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Tight clusters include T‑FC‑L1↔T‑FC‑L2 and H‑SL‑L2↔H‑SL‑L3, while H‑SL‑L1, X‑MC‑L3, and H‑OS‑L1 are analytically distinct.
• Confidence: Drift metrics are robust (based on >2 runs), but the small sample sizes for many protocols temper certainty.
